title: ferray-core: Element trait is sealed — no user-defined element types
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

NumPy allows user-defined dtypes via the new DType API (NEP 42/43). ferray-core's Element trait is sealed via a private Sealed supertrait — downstream crates cannot implement it for custom types. This is a deliberate safety choice but it means users can't store domain-specific value types (e.g., fixed-point numbers, quantized int8 with scale/zero-point, physical units) in ferray arrays without forking the crate. Research: (1) could Element be unsealed with a safe contract, (2) would an 'opaque' element type that just stores N bytes work, (3) is FerrayRecord sufficient for all custom-type use cases.
